title: maughania nilgheriensis
intro: Maughania nilgheriensis, also known as Kurchi or Kola Nut tree, is a deciduous tree native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Celastraceae family. This tree has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Maughania nilgheriensis include treating digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the bark and seeds of the tree are typically used to make decoctions, teas, and powders, which are consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
